clinical_criteria: Alcohol consumption <30 g/day for men and <20 g/day for women
Evidence of hepatic steatosis by imaging or histology in biopsy report
Fatty acid content exceeds 5% of liver weight
Text positive for diagnostic phrases including: steatosis, steatohepatitis, non-alcoholic fatty liver disease, NAFLD, NASH, fatty liver
Exclusion when medication use exceeds 3 months for specified medications
Histologically defined by macrovesicular steatosis, lobular inflammation, and hepatocellular ballooning
algorithm_summary: The phenotype algorithm defines two main cases: NAFLD (Cases 1 & 2) and ALD (Case 3). NAFLD cases require inclusion ICD-9 codes (571.5, 571.8, 571.9) or ICD-10 codes (K75.81, K76.0, K76.9), evidence of hepatic steatosis by imaging/histology or clinical notes with diagnostic text, and exclusion of patients with specified diagnoses and medications. ALD cases require inclusion ICD-9 codes (571.0, 571.1, 571.2, 571.3) or ICD-10 K70.* codes. Controls are patients without liver disease codes (570-579.99 for ICD-9, K70-K77 for ICD-10). The algorithm also includes a Case 4 for alcohol dependence without liver disorder. All cases collect covariate data including demographics, lab values (AST, ALT, bilirubin, LDH, ALP), anthropometric measures, and associated conditions like diabetes and obesity.
